source.title: Goal representations and motivational drive in schizophrenia: the role of prefrontal-striatal interactions

confidence_rationale: Strong clinical support that sustained goal representations are necessary for purposive behavior and cannot be replaced by local liking or simple harm avoidance. This supports the need for preserved goal traces when local routes fail.
failure_signatures: If intact local liking were sufficient for sustained goal pursuit, explicit unresolved-goal traces would add little architectural value.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Negative symptoms reflect a failure to maintain and use internal goal representations to drive behavior.
mapping.ree_translation: Ghost-goal search is one way to preserve goal-directed pressure through local invalidation rather than letting the agent collapse into passivity.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Clinical motivation failure does not specify a hippocampal storage format by itself.
mapping.source_context: Human clinical and neuroimaging review of schizophrenia negative symptoms.

confidence_components.notes: Useful existence proof for the necessity of persistent goal representations.
